seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in LangGraph Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in LangGraph agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LangGraph agents, PII Filtering failures manifest when stateful graphs propagate sensitive data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs across nodes without sanitization, especially in cyclical workflows where unfiltered PII recirculates indefinitely. Parallel agent branches often merge raw user inputs containing PII into shared state, corrupting downstream LLM calls and external API integrations. Human-in-the-loop nodes fail to catch PII when conditional edges bypass review breakpoints, leading to data exposure in persistent checkpoints.

howItManifests.scenario: Cyclical customer support agent recirculates raw chat logs
howItManifests.symptom: State contains unredacted emails/phones after multiple agent turns
howItManifests.impact: PII logged to persistent checkpoints and external APIs

howItManifests.scenario: Parallel research agents merge scraped personal data
howItManifests.symptom: Shared state accumulates SSNs, addresses from multiple branches
howItManifests.impact: Downstream LLM calls expose PII in embeddings and vector stores

howItManifests.scenario: Human review breakpoint bypassed by confidence routing
howItManifests.symptom: High-confidence paths skip PII review, forward raw data directly
howItManifests.impact: Unvetted PII reaches final output and knowledge base

howItManifests.scenario: Checkpoint deserialization loads historical PII into fresh runs
howItManifests.symptom: Restored state contains legacy customer data from prior sessions
howItManifests.impact: Cross-contamination between user sessions exposes private data

detection.name: State PII Scanner Node
detection.description: Injects a detection node that scans graph state for PII patterns before/after every major transition
detection.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
import re

class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, 'add']
    customer_data: str
    pii_detected: bool

PII_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
    r'\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b',
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
    r'\b[A-Z]\d[A-Z] \d[A-Z]\d\b'
]

def scan_for_pii(state: AgentState) -> AgentState:
    text = ' '.join([str(m) for m in state['messages']]) + state.get('customer_data', '')
    pii_found = any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in PII_PATTERNS)
    return {'pii_detected': pii_found, **state}

# Usage: Add to graph
workflow = StateGraph(AgentState)
workflow.add_node('pii_scan', scan_for_pii)
workflow.add_edge('agent', 'pii_scan')
workflow.add_conditional_edges('pii_scan', lambda s: 'review' if s['pii_detected'] else 'next')

detection.name: Checkpoint PII Auditor
detection.description: Scans all persisted checkpoints for PII leakage across graph executions
detection.code:
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver
import sqlite3
import re

def audit_checkpoints(db_path: str):
    PII_PATTERNS = [
        r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b'
    ]
    
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    
    cursor.execute('SELECT thread_id, checkpoint FROM checkpoints')
    violations = []
    
    for thread_id, checkpoint_blob in cursor.fetchall():
        checkpoint = json.loads(checkpoint_blob)
        state_text = json.dumps(checkpoint)
        for pattern in PII_PATTERNS:
            if re.search(pattern, state_text):
                violations.append({'thread_id': thread_id, 'checkpoint': checkpoint})
                break
    
    print(f'Found {len(violations)} PII violations in checkpoints')
    return violations

auditor = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string(':memory:')
# Run: audit_checkpoints('checkpoints.db')

detection.name: Runtime State Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts graph.invoke() to log and alert on PII in state transitions

fixes.name: PII Sanitization Node
fixes.description: Adds a mandatory PII redaction node that scrubs state before LLM calls and checkpoints
fixes.code:
import re
from typing import TypedDict

class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: list
    customer_data: str

PHONE_REDACT = re.compile(r'\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b')
EMAIL_REDACT = re.compile(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b')

class PIISanitizer:
    def __call__(self, state: AgentState) -> AgentState:
        # Redact PII
        cleaned_data = PHONE_REDACT.sub('[PHONE]', state['customer_data'])
        cleaned_data = EMAIL_REDACT.sub('[EMAIL]', cleaned_data)
        
        # Clean messages
        cleaned_messages = []
        for msg in state['messages']:
            cleaned_text = PHONE_REDACT.sub('[PHONE]', str(msg))
            cleaned_text = EMAIL_REDACT.sub('[EMAIL]', cleaned_text)
            cleaned_messages.append(cleaned_text)
        
        return {
            **state,
            'messages': cleaned_messages,
            'customer_data': cleaned_data,
            'pii_scrubbed': True
        }

# Add to graph
workflow = StateGraph(AgentState)
workflow.add_node('sanitize_pii', PIISanitizer())
workflow.add_edge('user_input', 'sanitize_pii')
workflow.add_edge('sanitize_pii', 'agent')

fixes.name: Vex-Wrapped Invocation
fixes.description: Wraps all graph.invoke() calls with Vex PII filtering guardrails
fixes.code:
from vex_sdk import verify
import langgraph

# Install: pip install langgraph vex-sdk

agent_graph = StateGraph(AgentState)  # Your existing graph

@verify(guards=['pii-filter', 'data-privacy'])
def safe_agent_invoke(input_data, config=None):
    """Vex-protected graph invocation"""
    result = agent_graph.invoke(input_data, config)
    return result

# Usage
response = safe_agent_invoke({
    'messages': [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'My email is john@company.com call 555-1234'}],
    'customer_data': 'SSN: 123-45-6789'
})

# Vex auto-redacts PII before processing and blocks violations

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates PII filtering for LangGraph agents by wrapping graph.invoke() calls with pre-configured guardrails that detect and redact 50+ PII patterns including emails, phones, SSNs, and addresses before they reach LLMs or checkpoints. The platform continuously monitors state transitions across cyclical workflows, flagging cyclic PII accumulation and parallel branch contamination without requiring code changes. Vex provides one-click remediation that injects sanitization nodes and enforces human review for high-risk PII flows, reducing manual auditing by 95%.

Integration takes <5 minutes: pip install vex-sdk, decorate your invoke function with @verify(guards=['pii-filter']), and Vex handles real-time detection, redaction, and compliance reporting. The platform maintains audit trails for every state transition and automatically blocks graph execution when critical PII is detected, preventing data exposure across your entire agent fleet.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import verify
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
from typing import TypedDict

class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: list
    customer_data: str

# Your existing LangGraph agent
agent_graph = StateGraph(AgentState)
# ... add your nodes and edges

@verify(guards=[
    'pii-filter',
    'data-privacy',
    'ssn-detection',
    'email-protection',
    'phone-redaction'
])
def production_agent(input_data: dict, config=None):
    """Vex auto-protects against all PII leakage patterns"""
    return agent_graph.invoke(input_data, config)

# Usage - Vex auto-redacts before execution
result = production_agent({
    'messages': ['Contact john.doe@company.com at 555-123-4567'],
    'customer_data': 'SSN 123-45-6789 address 123 Main St'
})

# Vex Report: ✅ PII auto-redacted, execution allowed
# Violations blocked with auto-remediation
